title: ferray-linalg: lstsq computes full SVD just for rank/singular values
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

lstsq() in solve.rs calls crate::decomp::svd(a, false) to get singular values for rank computation, but then separately uses QR for the actual solve via qr_decomp.solve_lstsq(). This means the input matrix is decomposed twice (QR + SVD). NumPy uses LAPACK's dgelsd which computes the least-squares solution and singular values in one pass using SVD. Either use SVD for both (like NumPy) or compute only singular values (svdvals) for the rank estimation without full U/Vt.
